latex: The first three laws above (x^{1} = x, x^{0} = 1 and x^{-1} = 1/x) are just part of the natural sequence of exponents. Have a look at this:
With x^{m}x^{n}, how many times do we end up multiplying "x"? Answer: first "m" times, then by another "n" times, for a total of "m+n" times.
